page: Google Cloud Platform n4d-highmem-96 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance belonging to the n4d server family. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs based on the x86_64 CPU architecture, paired with 768.0 GB of system memory. This configuration yields a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU, aligning it with memory-intensive enterprise workloads.

This instance does not include local NVMe SSD storage, meaning all storage operations must rely on external network-attached block storage. Additionally, the configuration features zero integrated GPUs, making it unsuitable for hardware-accelerated graphics or GPU-bound machine learning training. There are also zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses included with the instance.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the n4d-highmem-96 offers a high density of dedicated compute resources and RAM, which minimizes overhead for large-scale deployments compared to running multiple smaller instances. It is designed for workloads requiring substantial memory capacity and high CPU core counts, such as large relational databases, in-memory caching layers, enterprise resource planning systems, and memory-heavy data processing pipelines.
description: Google Cloud Platform n4d-highmem-96 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine within the n4d server family, engineered for memory-intensive workloads. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs and 768.0 GB of RAM, providing a high resource density of 8.0 GB of memory per core. The instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, requiring external block storage for data persistence. This hardware profile is optimized for large-scale database deployments, in-memory data stores, and enterprise applications that demand significant memory capacity alongside substantial compute power. By consolidating resources into a single high-capacity instance, it offers a structured approach to managing memory-bound enterprise workloads without the overhead of multi-instance clustering.
